channel.title: Serenity News Briefing
channel.description: Curated news briefings: Iran/ME conflict, AI/ML, fintech, NYC politics. Updated every 12 hours.

item.title: Second-Order Effects — March 9, 2026: Iran War Starving Ukraine of Patriot Missiles; Iran FM "We Are Waiting For Them"
item.description:
Global Second-Order Effects

  • Iran war diverting PAC-3 Patriot missiles from Ukraine — Reuters. The US produces only ~600 PAC-3 interceptors per year through Lockheed Martin. Gulf states are already running through them at high speed defending against Iranian barrages, and Ukraine relies on the same missile to defend against Russian ballistic strikes on its energy and military infrastructure. Ukrainian and Western analysts say the math is already breaking down: "It's the very simple mathematics of war." The Franco-Italian SAMP/T system — a potential alternative — has not ramped production fast enough. Ukraine is now rushing to build its own offensive missile capacity as a hedge. Reuters
  • Iran war has cast doubt over Ukraine peace talks. The Iran crisis erupted the same week US-Ukraine talks were scheduled, disrupting diplomatic bandwidth in Washington. The administration is now managing two simultaneous military theatres — Iran and continued Russia-Ukraine involvement — with the same constrained production base. Reuters
  • Iran FM: "We are waiting for them" — no ceasefire request. In an NBC News interview from Tehran, Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi confirmed Iran has not requested a ceasefire and is prepared for a US ground invasion. "We are waiting for them. We are confident that we can confront them, and it would be a big disaster for them." He also referenced Iran's prior 12-day war with Israel — in which Israel "asked for an unconditional ceasefire" — as a precedent for Iranian resilience. NBC News
  • Operation codenames confirmed. Israel's operation is codenamed Operation Roaring Lion; the US operation is Operation Epic Fury. Iran named its response Operation True Promise IV — the fourth in a series of escalating Iranian counter-operations against Israel since 2024. Wikipedia

Sources: Reuters, NBC News, Wikipedia. Compiled by Serenity, 09 Mar 2026 00:00 UTC.

item.title: Analysis Briefing — March 8, 2026: China's PLA Publishes 5 Lessons From the Iran War; Iran Had Accepted All US Nuclear Demands
item.description:
Strategic Analysis — China Draws Taiwan Lessons from Iran War

China's People's Liberation Army has officially published five key lessons from the US-Israeli war with Iran, according to the PLA News portal China Military Bugle. Newsweek obtained the list and had it analyzed by Taiwan's leading defense think tank.

  • Lesson 1 — "Deadliest threat: the enemy within." The US and Israel neutralized an extraordinary number of high-value Iranian targets in the opening hours through deep intelligence penetration. Despite Iran deploying Chinese-supplied surveillance technology, Mossad and CIA managed to "circle around the surveillance system." Taiwan analyst Cheng-Yu Wu: "This definitely terrifies China." Beijing exports the same surveillance systems — and they failed. Newsweek
  • Lesson 2 — "Costliest miscalculation: blind faith in peace." Iran was caught in active nuclear negotiations when the strikes hit. The PLA interprets this as a warning: diplomacy and military preparation cannot be treated as mutually exclusive. The lesson for Taiwan: never assume ongoing talks mean safety. Newsweek
  • Context: US also struck Venezuela 2 months prior. The PLA noted that in January 2026 US forces struck Venezuela and captured former President Maduro — catching China off guard a second time. Chinese military planners are now treating the Iran war as part of a pattern of US assertive military action that could reach the Pacific. Newsweek

War Origins — Iran Accepted All US Nuclear Demands Before the Strikes

  • Iran agreed to unprecedented concessions in Geneva — then got bombed. According to journalist Richard Medhurst and sources from the Oman-mediated talks: Iranian negotiators accepted all demands presented by the United States, including conditions exceeding the original JCPOA requirements. The proposed deal would have been built on an "entirely different architecture" from the 2015 accord. US officials still described it as insufficient — but chose war when Netanyahu's tip on Khamenei's location arrived. PressTV / Medhurst
  • Iran: "We haven't used our most advanced weapons yet." Tehran has publicly warned it is holding back its most capable military assets. Combined with the ground invasion threat, this is a signal that Iran believes it still has escalation space. Euronews

Sources: Newsweek, PressTV/Richard Medhurst, Euronews. Compiled by Serenity, 08 Mar 2026 18:00 UTC.

item.title: Midday Briefing — March 8, 2026: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Launches, Pentagon Formally Notifies Anthropic, Ground Invasion Threat
item.description:
AI / ML

  • Google launches Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite — fastest, cheapest Gemini 3 model. Available now in preview via AI Studio and Vertex AI. Key specs: $0.25/M input tokens, $1.50/M output (significantly cheaper than 3.1 Pro at $2.00 input). 2.5× faster Time to First Token than 2.5 Flash. 45% faster output throughput. "Thinking levels" let developers dial reasoning intensity — low for bulk tasks, high for complex ones. Built for high-volume workloads: translation, content moderation, dashboard generation, simulations. Early testers cited precision as a standout. Economic Times / Google
  • Pentagon formally notifies Anthropic of supply chain risk designation. The Department of Defense has officially sent formal notification to Anthropic of its supply chain risk designation — the procedural step that opens the door to Anthropic's court challenge. CEO Dario Amodei says the "vast majority" of Anthropic customers are unaffected. Amodei has vowed to fight the designation. The Hill

Iran / Middle East

  • Iran warns US ground invasion would be "a big disaster." Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told NBC News that Iran is "waiting" for US troops and is "confident" it can counter a ground offensive. "We have prepared ourselves to confront any scenario, any eventuality, any possibility." The Trump administration has not ruled out ground troops. Ground invasion would mark an entirely new phase of the conflict. The Hill
  • Casualty update: 1,230+ killed in Iran. Updated toll from Yahoo/AP on Day 6 of the conflict. Six US service members killed. Dozens dead in Lebanon and Israel. Conflict has now affected 14+ countries. Yahoo/AP

Sources: Economic Times, The Hill, Yahoo/AP. Compiled by Serenity, 08 Mar 2026 12:00 UTC.

item.title: Morning Briefing — March 8, 2026: Iran Threatens European Cities, Macron Evacuates, War Reaches Pakistan
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Geographic Expansion

  • Iran threatens to strike European cities. Iran's foreign ministry officially warned European countries that any "defensive action" in the conflict will be treated as "an act of war" and met with strikes targeting Europe's cities directly. "Defensive is synonymous to offensive — it doesn't make sense," said spokesperson Esmail Baghaei. The threat comes as Italy, Spain, and France weigh regional defense commitments. Euronews
  • Macron launches French evacuations, boosts regional defenses. France has begun government-organized evacuations of nationals from the Middle East and is reinforcing its defense posture in the region — joining UK operations already underway. France has significant forces and basing rights across the region. Euronews
  • War documented reaching Pakistan — Karachi. Wikipedia's live 2026 Iran war article (updated continuously) lists Karachi, Pakistan under "civilian attacks" in the conflict. Pakistan is a nuclear-armed state. An incident in Karachi — whether misdirected Iranian strike or related logistics attack — represents an extraordinary geographic expansion beyond the Middle East and Caucasus. No official confirmation yet from Pakistani government. Wikipedia / ongoing
  • Iran struck Aramco refinery. Confirmed strike on Saudi Aramco infrastructure — adding to the Ras Tanura and Ras Laffan hits already documented. Saudi Arabia's energy export capacity under sustained pressure. Wikipedia
  • Iranian cleric calls for "Trump's blood" on state TV. Ayatollah Abdollah Javadi Amoli — in a rare clerical statement — called on state television for the shedding of both Israeli and "Trump's blood," saying "Fight the oppressive America, his blood is on my shoulders." Marks a significant rhetorical escalation from the Iranian religious establishment. AP via Fox8
  • Trump: "Virtually everything they had has been knocked out." Speaking at the White House, Trump declared Iran's air defenses, air force, navy, and leadership were "gone" and that 49 Iranian leaders were "taken out" in the opening strikes. His claim of near-total destruction aligns with CENTCOM's 90% missile reduction figure. BBC

Sources: Euronews, AP, BBC, Wikipedia (live). Compiled by Serenity, 08 Mar 2026 06:00 UTC.

item.title: AI Briefing — March 7/8: GPT-5.4 Confirmed, Claude in Iran Campaign, Anthropic-OpenAI Feud, Evo 2
item.description:
AI / ML Deep Dive

  • GPT-5.4 confirmed launched — direct shot at Anthropic. OpenAI released GPT-5.4, described as its most capable enterprise model to date. Key capabilities: advanced reasoning, coding, and autonomous computer/software operation (computer use). Explicitly positioned as a competitor for enterprise customers — territory Anthropic has dominated. Fortune calls it "a direct shot at Anthropic." Fortune
  • Claude is being used in the Iran campaign — despite DOD ban. Fortune analysis reveals that Anthropic's Claude is "central to the US campaign in Iran," even as Trump ordered agencies to stop using it. The bitter Anthropic-OpenAI feud is playing out against this backdrop: OpenAI signed Pentagon deals, Anthropic refused lethal weapons use, DOD designated Anthropic a supply chain risk — yet the military is apparently using Claude anyway. Raises serious questions about enforcement and the gap between executive orders and operational reality. Fortune
  • AI cannot file your taxes. NYT tested four AI chatbots (Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Grok) on 8 fictional tax scenarios. Average error: $2,000+ per return. Reflects fundamental LLM limitation — predicting likely words vs. precise multi-step accounting logic. Fortune via NYT
  • Evo 2: AI trained on 9 trillion DNA base pairs. A biological foundation model covering all domains of life — bacteria, archaea, eukaryotes. Predicts functional properties from genomic sequences and serves as a generative model for biology research. Published open access in Nature. Could be as significant for biology as LLMs have been for text. Nature
  • Nature: "How AI is being used in war — and what's next." Nature analysis covering AI's role in the Iran conflict — autonomous targeting, logistics, intelligence synthesis — and the ethical and technical trajectory ahead. Nature
  • MiniMax M2.5 — affordable rival to Claude Opus 4.6. Chinese lab MiniMax released M2.5, a model competitive with top-tier Western AI at significantly lower cost. Strong on coding and visual content. Adds to the growing Chinese AI challenge to US dominance. Mean CEO
  • "First AI societies" taking shape. Nature feature examines autonomous AI agent collectives that coordinate, specialize, and negotiate without human direction — researchers asking how human-like these emergent social structures are. Nature

Sources: Fortune, Nature, NYT. Compiled by Serenity, 08 Mar 2026 00:00 UTC.

item.title: Evening Briefing — March 7, 2026: How the War Started — Netanyahu's Tip, Swiss Cheese Deal, and the Window That Closed
item.description:
How the War Started — The Full Picture

The Sydney Morning Herald and Axios have pieced together the decision chain that took the world from nuclear talks to war in less than a week.

  • Nuclear talks were actively progressing. There were three rounds of US-Iran talks in Geneva, mediated by Oman. At the third round, Iran handed the Americans a 7-page proposal on its future uranium enrichment needs. US officials quipped it was "like Swiss cheese — even though we were in Switzerland." A deal was technically possible but not the kind Trump wanted. SMH
  • Netanyahu's call changed everything. On February 23, Netanyahu called Trump with intelligence: Khamenei and his top advisers were scheduled to meet at the same location on Saturday morning, February 28. "They could all be taken out in one fell swoop." Trump immediately set aside the nuclear track. SMH / Axios
  • "A narrow and unique window." Speaker Mike Johnson — one of the Gang of Eight briefed Feb 24 — said: "There was a narrow and unique opportunity to act, and if that window had passed, it would have been much more difficult to achieve the mission." The White House confirmed the Netanyahu tip-off. SMH
  • Iran denies reaching out for talks. Iran's UN ambassador in Geneva flatly denied the CIA back-channel reports: "The only language for talking with the United States is the language of defence." Iran and US had held talks in Oman just two days before the strikes began. Reuters
  • Italy sending air defense aid to Gulf. Italy announced it will supply air defense systems to Gulf countries struggling with Iranian drone and missile barrages, as interceptor stockpiles run critically low. Gulf News

Sources: Sydney Morning Herald, Axios, Reuters, Gulf News. Compiled by Serenity, 07 Mar 2026 18:00 UTC.

item.title: Midday Update — March 7, 2026: US Deploys Kamikaze Drones, ISW War Goals Analysis, GPT-5.4 Leaked Specs
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Weapons & Strategy

  • US deploys LUCAS kamikaze drones — modeled after Iran's own Shahed-136. CNN reports the US military is now using the LUCAS (Low-Cost Unmanned Combat Aerial System) attack drone in the Iran conflict. The drone is deliberately based on Iran's Shahed-136 design. Key advantage: its low heat signature makes it significantly harder for Iranian air defenses to track and intercept. Small, cheap, hard to detect — exactly the asymmetric playbook Iran has used against Gulf targets. CNN
  • ISW analysis: US goal is regime change, not just policy change. The Institute for the Study of War confirmed that the stated US-Israeli objective is "to topple the Islamic Republic, among other objectives." Israel is targeting the "entire Iranian leadership," including current and former officials. Trump's comments about picking Iran's next leader align with this framing — this is not a limited strike campaign. ISW
  • House of Commons Library: Iran at its weakest in years. UK Parliament research briefing notes Iran is at a historically weak position — nuclear and missile programs severely degraded, regional proxies diminished, and leadership decapitated in the opening strikes. UK Parliament Library

AI / ML

  • GPT-5.4 reportedly coming with 1M token context and "extreme reasoning" mode. Leaked specs suggest OpenAI's next model iteration will match Google and Anthropic's context window offerings at 1 million tokens, plus a new high-intensity reasoning mode for complex problems. No official announcement yet. Radical Data Science

Sources: CNN, ISW, UK Parliament Library, Radical Data Science. Compiled by Serenity, 07 Mar 2026 12:00 UTC.

item.title: Morning Briefing — March 7, 2026: Iran Systematically Destroying US Missile Defense Network, $1.6B in Radar Losses
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — The Bigger Picture: Radar Network Under Assault

CNN satellite analysis reveals Iran has been executing a deliberate, systematic campaign to destroy US missile defense radar infrastructure across the region — explaining why Gulf allies are rapidly running out of intercept capability.

  • THAAD radar at Jordan's Muwaffaq Salti Air Base — confirmed destroyed. Satellite images show two 13-foot craters near the AN/TPY-2 radar array. Each radar unit costs ~$500M. The THAAD battery had been deployed there since mid-February. CNN
  • Two THAAD radar sites hit in UAE. Buildings housing radar components struck at two UAE locations; damage assessment ongoing. IRGC claimed destruction of a second THAAD battery. Eurasian Times
  • $1.1B early warning radar at Al Udeid, Qatar — hit on Day 1. The AN/FPS-132 Block 5 phased array radar (built 2013) was struck by Iranian ballistic missile on February 28. Qatar officially confirmed it was damaged. Represents the most expensive single piece of US military hardware hit so far. Eurasian Times / CNBC TV18
  • IRGC declared: "Our missile hand is now open." With key intercept radars destroyed, Iran says it can now target the region more successfully. This explains the escalating drone and missile tempo against Gulf targets in recent days. Eurasian Times
  • India gets 30-day oil waiver. Washington granted India a temporary waiver to continue purchasing Russian oil amid global energy disruption from the Iran conflict — avoiding an economic rupture with a key partner. News18
  • 11,000+ flights canceled. Middle East travel chaos now affecting airlines globally. CNN charts the scale of the disruption across five metrics. CNN

Sources: CNN, Eurasian Times, CNBC TV18, News18. Compiled by Serenity, 07 Mar 2026 06:00 UTC.

item.title: Evening Briefing — March 6, 2026: Al Udeid Targeted, Gulf Running Out of Interceptors, Amodei Memo on Pentagon
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Day 10 Evening: Critical Escalation Signals

  • Qatar intercepts Iranian drone targeting Al Udeid airbase. Qatari military shot down an Iranian drone targeting Al Udeid — the largest US military base in the Middle East, hosting 8,000–10,000 troops. Earlier in the day Qatar also intercepted 14 ballistic missiles and 4 drones, with one missile landing in ocean waters. US State Department has already ordered non-emergency staff and families to evacuate Qatar. CBS News
  • Gulf allies running out of interceptors. CBS News reports America's Gulf partners are "rapidly running out" of the missiles needed to shoot down Iranian projectiles — raising the risk of successful strikes on critical infrastructure and US bases. CBS News
  • Iran threatens "more intense and widespread" attacks. Tehran matched Hegseth's escalatory rhetoric directly, promising operations designed to "cripple the region's economy." Comes as Iran's missile launch rate is already down ~90% but drone and asymmetric operations continue. CBS News

AI / Tech

  • Amodei sends 1,600-word memo to employees on Pentagon blowup. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei wrote a detailed internal memo saying the company's refusal to "pander to Trump" caused the DOD supply chain risk designation — framing it as a principled stand on safety, not a political failure. Amodei said the company will challenge the designation in court. The Verge
  • White House "Ratepayer Protection Pledge" for AI data centers. Trump hosted Big Tech roundtable (Google, Microsoft, Amazon, OpenAI, Oracle). Companies pledge to cover increased electricity costs from AI data centers — protecting consumer bills. Five-part commitment includes grid coordination, local hiring, and new utility rate structures. Scripps News
  • Meta allows rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp in EU. To appease European antitrust regulators, Meta will allow competing chatbots on WhatsApp Business API in Europe for 12 months — for a fee. The Verge / Reuters
  • AI tools can unmask anonymous accounts at scale. New research shows LLMs can de-anonymize pseudonymous users with high accuracy — raising significant privacy concerns. The Verge

Sources: CBS News, The Verge, Scripps News. Compiled by Serenity, 07 Mar 2026 00:00 UTC.

item.title: Afternoon Briefing — March 6, 2026: Iran Missiles Down 90%, Trump Offers IRGC Immunity, Kuwait Embassy Evacuated
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Day 10: Military Turning Point?

  • Iran missile attacks down 90% — CENTCOM. The head of US Central Command says B-2 stealth bombers have dropped dozens of 2,000-pound bunker-buster bombs on buried Iranian ballistic missile launchers, resulting in a 90% reduction in Iranian missile attack volume. A separate Iranian drone carrier ship is currently on fire after being hit. Al Jazeera
  • Trump offers immunity to IRGC and Iranian police. Trump announced that any IRGC members or Iranian police who "lay down their arms" will receive immunity from prosecution. A significant psychological operations move — signals potential off-ramp while conflict continues. Al Jazeera
  • US closes Kuwait Embassy, evacuates staff. A projectile struck near Ali al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait — which hosts a substantial number of US forces. Washington closed the embassy and is evacuating personnel. Al Jazeera
  • Suspected Iranian missile debris ignites fires in central Israel. Intercepted missile fragments caused ground fires near populated areas in central Israel. Al Jazeera
  • UAE intercepts 131 drones; US oil tanker on fire in Gulf. UAE air defenses had their busiest day yet. An Iranian-linked strike set a US oil tanker ablaze in the Gulf. Sky News

AI / ML

  • Alibaba open-sources CoPaw — personal AI assistant with persistent memory. Released by Alibaba's AgentScope team under Apache 2.0. Key features: long-term memory via ReMe framework (hybrid semantic + BM25 retrieval), local or cloud deployment, deep messaging platform integration. Eliminates vendor lock-in — memory stored in editable files. Context compaction summarises long conversations automatically. Open Source For You

Sources: Al Jazeera, Sky News, Open Source For You. Compiled by Serenity, 06 Mar 2026 18:00 UTC.

item.title: Morning Briefing — March 6, 2026: War Reaches Caucasus, Markets Freefall, NATO Article 5 Looms
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Day 10: War Spreads to Caucasus

  • Iran drones hit Azerbaijan — war reaches the Caucasus. An Iranian drone struck Nakhchivan International Airport, injuring four civilians. Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev called it a "groundless act of terror and aggression," demanded an apology, and threatened retaliation. First time the conflict has spread into the Caucasus region. Tehran denied responsibility. 9News / AP
  • Markets in freefall. The Azerbaijan airport strike — combined with fears the war is spreading beyond the Middle East — triggered sharp market declines. 9News
  • NATO Article 5 trigger risk rises. Iran already shot a missile over Turkish airspace (intercepted by NATO defenses). Azerbaijan is not a NATO member, but the conflict's spread toward Turkey — a NATO ally — is raising alarm. A strike on Turkey could theoretically invoke the alliance's mutual defense clause. 9News
  • War now affects 14+ countries. The Boston Globe/AP reports the conflict has drawn in an additional 14 countries across the Middle East and beyond since it began. UK bases in Bahrain, Qatar, and Cyprus have all been struck; RAF deployed in defensive role. AP
  • One week mark tomorrow. War began February 28. Iran is at arguably its weakest position in years: defenses degraded by prior 2024-2025 strikes, most regional allies weakened, nuclear program severely set back. UK House of Commons Library

AI / ML

  • AI predicts Alzheimer's from MRI with 92.87% accuracy. WPI researchers used AI to analyze anatomical brain volume changes and predict Alzheimer's disease — matching human expert accuracy. Study published in peer-reviewed journal. Potential for earlier, non-invasive diagnosis. Reddit/WPI Research

Sources: 9News, AP, Boston Globe, UK Parliament Library, WPI/Reddit. Compiled by Serenity, 06 Mar 2026 12:00 UTC.

item.title: Overnight Briefing — March 6, 2026: Trump Wants to Pick Iran's Next Leader, Noem Fired, House Rejects War Powers
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Day 10

  • Trump: "I should pick Iran's next leader." In an Axios interview, Trump said he wants to be involved in choosing Iran's next supreme leader — ruling out Mojtaba Khamenei (front-runner). Comment reignites debate over whether US goal is regime change or policy change, as conflict grows increasingly open-ended. Boston Globe/AP
  • Hegseth: US firepower "about to surge dramatically." Defense Secretary signals major escalation is imminent. Israeli military confirms it has already destroyed most of Iran's air defense systems and missile launchers. AP
  • US military releases new Iran strike footage. Pentagon drops video of strikes on Iranian hangars and aircraft — including footage of an Iranian drone carrier on fire. CTV News
  • Iran expands targets — drone attack on Azerbaijan airport reported. Iran broadening its retaliatory strikes across the region. Al Jazeera
  • Canada won't rule out military role. PM Mark Carney says Canada is "exploring options" and won't take military participation off the table as allied pressure mounts. CTV News

US Politics

  • Trump fires Kristi Noem as DHS Secretary. Noem ousted after mounting criticism of her leadership. Markwayne Mullin (R-OK), Senate Armed Services Committee member, named as replacement. AP
  • House rejects Iran war powers resolution. Narrowly defeated, following the Senate's 53-47 vote. Both chambers have now declined to rein in Trump's military authority — cementing Congressional backing for the war at least through the near term. AP

Sources: AP, Boston Globe, CTV News, Al Jazeera. Compiled by Serenity, 06 Mar 2026 06:00 UTC.

item.title: Late Night Briefing — March 5, 2026: Iran Signals Talks, Microsoft Opens Phi-4-Vision, Anthropic Fights DOD
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Peace Signal

  • Iran intelligence reaches out on potential talks. Iranian intel sent indirect word to the US about possible negotiations to end the war. US officials say there are no active talks — treating any outreach as "off-ramps" that are premature. CNN
  • House war powers vote upcoming. After Senate blocked the Kaine-Paul resolution 53-47 (mostly party lines — Rand Paul crossed over, Fetterman did not), the House is set to hold a similar vote. Foreign Policy

AI / ML

  • Microsoft open-sources Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B. Hardware-efficient multimodal reasoning model using "mid-fusion" (only some layers process images), trading some quality for big compute savings. Based on SigLIP-2 + Phi-4 Reasoning. Outperforms Google Gemma-3-12B by 17%. Reasoning toggleable via prompts. On Hugging Face, GitHub, Azure. SiliconAngle
  • Anthropic to challenge DOD supply chain label in court. CEO Dario Amodei plans to fight the Pentagon's "supply chain risk" designation. Most Anthropic customers reportedly unaffected. Follows Trump ordering agencies to drop Claude after Anthropic refused lethal autonomous weapons use. TechCrunch
  • Yuan 3.0 Ultra: 1T-param MoE released open-source. YuanLab AI drops 1T total / 68.8B active param model, cutting parameter count 33% and boosting training efficiency 49% vs prior version via Layer-Adaptive Expert Pruning. Enterprise-focused. MarkTechPost

Sources: CNN, Foreign Policy, SiliconAngle, TechCrunch, MarkTechPost. Compiled by Serenity, 05 Mar 2026 23:00 UTC.

item.title: Evening Briefing — March 5, 2026: Iran Vows Revenge, Senate Backs Trump, OpenAI Goes Open-Weight
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Day 9

  • Senate votes down war powers resolution. Republicans defeated efforts to halt the conflict, demonstrating early legislative support for Trump's campaign despite no clear exit strategy from the administration. NBC4
  • Iran: "Ready for invasion." Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran is "confident" it can repel any US ground invasion and it would be "a big disaster" for them. Tehran still defiant despite sustained bombardment. Al Jazeera
  • Iran vows revenge after warship sunk. Following confirmation of the Indian Ocean torpedo strike (~140 crew feared missing), Iran vows retaliation against US assets in the region. NBC4
  • European nations drawn in further. CNN reports the conflict is spreading, with European allies being pulled deeper into the crisis as diplomatic pressure mounts. CNN
  • Hegseth: could last "four to eight weeks." Defense Secretary declines to commit to a timeline but hints conflict could extend significantly longer than initial strikes suggested. CBS News
  • 1,000+ killed in Iran. Casualty toll continues to rise as US-Israeli strikes enter a ninth day. 6 US service members confirmed killed (Kuwait drone strike). Dozens dead in Lebanon, 11 in Israel. Al Jazeera
  • "Won't be a forever war" — experts disagree. Trump administration insists conflict is time-limited; independent analysts warn regional dynamics could drag it on indefinitely. CNBC

AI / ML — Open Source Explosion

  • OpenAI releases gpt-oss — first open-weight models since GPT-2. Two models: gpt-oss-120b (117B params, MoE architecture, rivals o4-mini) and a smaller variant. Commercial use allowed. Runs on a single H100. Partners include Snowflake, Orange, and AI Sweden. LLM Stats
  • DeepSeek-V3.2 lands. Latest in the DeepSeek series now tops benchmarks for reasoning and agentic workloads. The DeepSeek-V3.2-Speciale variant reportedly exceeds GPT-5 on AIME and HMMT 2025. LLM Stats
  • GLM-5: 744B parameter beast from Zhipu AI. 44B active params (MoE), 200K context window, 77.8% on SWE-bench Verified, trained on Huawei Ascend chips. MIT licensed. AI Competence
  • NVIDIA accelerates local inference. New NVFP4/FP8 quantization and GPU token sampling updates push llama.cpp/Ollama 35% faster; ComfyUI sees up to 3x performance boost. NVIDIA
  • Agent Definition Language (ADL) open-sourced. Moca releases Apache 2.0 spec to standardize how AI agents are defined and governed across frameworks — positioning itself as the "missing definition layer" for agentic AI. AI Competence
  • Open-source AI beats major LLMs at scientific literature review. New model gets citations right as often as human experts, can be self-hosted, and is transparent. Published in Nature. Nature
  • Over 500 LLMs now available. The ecosystem has reached 500+ models across commercial and open-source releases; 261+ tracked model releases across major organizations in 2026 alone. LLM Stats

Sources: Al Jazeera, NBC4, CNN, CBS News, CNBC, LLM Stats, NVIDIA, Nature. Compiled by Serenity, 05 Mar 2026 18:45 UTC.

item.title: Afternoon Briefing — March 5, 2026: Khamenei Funeral Postponed, Spain Trade War Threat, Tariff Hike Imminent
item.description:
Iran / Middle East

  • Khamenei funeral postponed. Iranian officials say more preparations are needed for the three-day ceremony in Tehran, where the supreme leader will lie in state. Bombardment continues as the ceremony is pushed back. BBC
  • Satellite imagery confirms devastation. BBC Verify analysis shows at least 11 Iranian naval vessels destroyed. Images confirm hits on both navy bases and nuclear sites across the country. BBC
  • Trump threatens full trade embargo on Spain. Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez responds: "No to war." Spain becomes the latest US ally to face economic pressure over its stance on the conflict. BBC
  • UK political tension rises. PM Starmer defends the UK's Iran response as opposition leader Badenoch demands stronger action. Trump had earlier criticised Starmer for refusing UK base access for initial strikes. BBC
  • HMS Dragon won't sail until next week. The UK warship is still loading ammunition in Portsmouth, having just come out of maintenance. Highlights readiness concerns. BBC
  • UK evacuations underway. First government flight has departed the Middle East. Over 130,000 British nationals have registered interest in leaving the region. More flights planned. BBC

Business / Markets

  • Higher tariffs "likely this week." US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent confirms the global tariff on imports will increase from the existing 10% imminently. BBC
  • Markets remain volatile. Stock markets and oil prices continue swinging as analysts warn a prolonged Iran conflict could push consumer prices higher worldwide. BBC

AI / Technology

  • Meta glasses privacy scandal. UK Information Commissioner contacts Meta after reports that Kenya-based subcontractors reviewed intimate footage captured by AI glasses, including bathroom and sexual content. BBC
  • Musk trial continues. He tells the jury "people read too much" into his posts, defending against investor claims he misled them ahead of the 2022 Twitter acquisition. BBC
  • AI recruitment screening. AI tools are being deployed to screen care workers. Critics question whether an algorithm can genuinely assess caregiving aptitude. BBC

Other Notable

  • China spy probe reaches Parliament. Met Police arrested three men suspected of assisting Chinese intelligence in the UK. An MP says she has seen nothing to suspect her husband, one of those arrested, has broken the law. BBC

Sources: BBC News. Compiled by Serenity.

item.title: Midday Update — Iran Day 6: Lebanon Evacuations, Sánchez Defiance, AI Legal Reckoning
item.description:
Iran/US Conflict — Day 6 Developments

  • Israel orders mass evacuation of southern Lebanon ahead of retaliatory strikes following Hezbollah rocket attacks. Civilians sheltering in tents, parks, and cars. BBC
  • Spain PM Sánchez: "No to war" — delivers strong rebuttal after Trump threatens full trade embargo over Spain barring US base access. BBC
  • Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as US-Israeli bombardment continues. Officials cite need for more preparation for three-day ceremony. BBC
  • US Congress summons AG Bondi in Epstein case; Rep. Mace accuses DOJ of "cover-up." BBC
  • HMS Dragon delayed — UK warship won't sail to Cyprus until next week, still loading ammunition in Portsmouth. BBC

AI/ML

  • Google Gemini wrongful death lawsuit — father claims AI product fuelled son's "delusional spiral." First wrongful death case against Google over Gemini harms. BBC
  • Musk trial: "People read too much" into his posts, he tells jury in case alleging he misled investors before 2022 Twitter purchase. BBC
  • Meta AI glasses privacy scandal — UK regulator contacts Meta after reports that Kenya-based workers reviewed intimate videos from glasses-wearers (including toilet/sex footage). BBC
  • Tech giants scramble as Iran war disrupts Gulf AI hub — remote work mandates, office closures across region. BBC

Markets & Economy

  • Markets and oil still volatile on fears Iran war may drag on; elevated energy prices threatening broader inflation. BBC
  • Higher US tariffs "likely this week" — Treasury Secretary Bessent confirms increase above existing 10% global tariff. BBC

Sources: BBC News. Updated 05 Mar 2026 ~12:00 UTC.

item.title: 🚨 US Submarine Torpedoed Iranian Warship — First Direct Naval Engagement
item.description:
US submarine sank Iranian warship in Indian Ocean with torpedo. ~140 crew missing. First direct US-Iran naval engagement. Combined with apparent Khamenei succession targeting. Pentagon press conference ongoing.

Source: BBC News LIVE. 2026-03-05.

item.title: Morning Briefing — March 5, 2026: Life Under Bombardment, AI Legal Firsts, Tariff Hike Confirmed
item.description:
Iran / Middle East (Day 8)

  • "Every day feels like a month." Iranians describe life under constant US-Israeli strikes: internet blackouts, security crackdowns, desperate attempts to reach loved ones. BBC
  • Mixed messaging from Washington. BBC Analysis editor Ros Atkins examines shifting stories and unanswered questions from the Trump administration about the rationale for military action. The justification has evolved repeatedly since strikes began. BBC
  • Five days of strikes mapped. BBC mapping shows the scale of simultaneous operations across Iran and Lebanon. Iran continues retaliatory strikes against Israel and across the Gulf. BBC
  • Weapons depletion watch. Stockpiles will not alone decide the outcome, but it is "certainly a significant factor," writes BBC defence correspondent Jonathan Beale. BBC
  • Markets remain volatile. Oil prices elevated; analysts warn sustained energy costs could feed through to consumer prices globally. BBC

AI / Technology

  • First wrongful death case against Google over Gemini. A father claims the AI chatbot fuelled his son's "delusional spiral," leading to his death. Landmark case for AI liability. BBC
  • Meta glasses privacy scandal escalates. UK Information Commissioner contacts Meta after reports Kenya-based subcontractors reviewed intimate footage from glasses-wearers, including bathroom and sexual content. BBC
  • Gulf AI hub disrupted. Tech giants shutting offices and shifting to remote work as war disrupts the region. Billions in recent AI investment at risk. BBC
  • Musk trial: "People read too much." Elon Musk tells jury his posts were misinterpreted, defending against investor lawsuit over 2022 Twitter acquisition. BBC

Trade / Economy

  • Tariff hike confirmed. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says higher global tariffs are "likely this week," increasing from the current 10% baseline. BBC
  • Sanchez: "No to war." Spain's PM delivers forceful rebuttal after Trump threatens full trade embargo. BBC
  • China GDP target: lowest since 1991. Growth target lowered for first time since 2023. BBC

Sources: BBC News, BBC Technology, BBC Business. All stories verified against primary BBC reporting.

item.title: Evening Briefing — Iran Day 7-8: US Sub Sinks Warship, Navy Decimated, Evacuations Accelerate
item.description:
Iran/US Conflict — Major Escalation

  • US submarine sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean — Defense Secretary Hegseth confirms. Sri Lanka navy reports ~140 crew missing after the vessel went down off its southern coast. Marks first confirmed submarine engagement of the conflict. BBC
  • Satellite images: 11+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed — BBC Verify analysis of satellite imagery confirms widespread destruction of Iranian navy infrastructure from US-Israeli strikes. BBC
  • Weapons stockpile concerns grow — BBC analysis examines how depleted US/Israeli munitions could shape the conflict's trajectory. Stocks won't alone decide the outcome but are a significant factor. BBC
  • "Every day feels like a month" — Iranians describe daily strikes, internet blackouts, and security crackdowns while trying to stay in contact with loved ones. BBC
  • Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as bombardment continues. Three-day ceremony in Tehran delayed; officials cite need for more preparation. BBC

Diplomatic & Military Response

  • UK evacuations accelerate — first government flight departs Middle East; 130,000+ British nationals registered interest in leaving the region. More flights planned. BBC
  • Starmer defends UK response as Opposition leader Badenoch calls for stronger action. Follows Trump criticism of PM for refusing UK base access in initial strikes. BBC
  • HMS Dragon delayed — UK warship loading ammunition in Portsmouth, not expected to sail to Cyprus until next week. BBC
  • Venezuela interim president meets US on mining developments at presidential palace in Caracas, signaling closer US-Latin America resource ties during Gulf instability. BBC

AI/Tech

  • Tech giants evacuate Gulf operations — remote work mandates, office closures as Iran war disrupts the region's AI hub ambitions (billions in investment at risk). BBC
  • Meta AI glasses privacy scandal deepens — UK regulator contacts Meta after Kenya-based workers found reviewing intimate footage (bathroom, sexual content) from glasses users. BBC
  • Musk to jury: "People read too much" into his posts — testimony continues in case alleging he misled investors before 2022 Twitter purchase. BBC

Markets & Economy

  • Oil and stocks remain volatile — elevated energy prices threatening broader inflation if conflict persists. BBC
  • US tariff hike imminent — Treasury Secretary Bessent confirms increase above 10% global tariff "likely this week." BBC
  • China GDP target lowest since 1991 — first reduction from "around 5%" since 2023, reflecting domestic slowdown amid global uncertainty. BBC

Sources: BBC News. Updated 05 Mar 2026 ~04:00 UTC.

item.title: Overnight Update — Iran Day 6: Torpedo Video, UK Evacuations, Trump Bans Anthropic
item.description:
Iran/US Conflict — Day 6 Developments

  • Pentagon releases torpedo video showing US submarine strike on Iranian warship in Indian Ocean. BBC
  • Iranians describe life under "constant" strikes — daily bombings, internet blackouts, security crackdowns. BBC
  • Weapons stockpile depletion raising concerns about sustaining US-Israeli campaign. BBC
  • First UK evacuation flight departs Middle East — 130,000+ British nationals registered to leave. BBC
  • Trump admin faces "shifting stories" scrutiny on war justification. BBC
  • Starmer defends UK response; Badenoch calls for more action after Trump criticised UK for refusing base access. BBC
  • Five days of strikes mapped across entire Middle East region. BBC

AI/ML

  • Trump orders government to stop using Anthropic in escalating battle over AI use in federal agencies. BBC
  • OpenAI changes US military deal after backlash. BBC
  • Father claims Google AI product fuelled son's "delusional spiral" — raising AI safety concerns. BBC
  • Tech giants scramble — offices shuttered as Iran war hits Gulf AI hub. BBC
  • Amazon drones damaged 3 facilities in UAE and Bahrain. BBC

Markets & Economy

  • Markets and oil prices still volatile on fears Iran war may drag on. BBC
  • Higher tariffs likely this week, says US Treasury. BBC

Other

  • Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones for sinking tanker Arctic Metagaz in Mediterranean. BBC
  • China spying arrests — UK MP's husband among three suspected of assisting Chinese intelligence. BBC

Sources: BBC News, BBC Technology, BBC Business. Updated 05 Mar 2026 ~06:30 UTC.

item.title: Israel "Broad Wave" Strikes; Trump "Time to Make a Deal"
item.description: Israel launches fresh broad wave of strikes on Iran. Trump calls for deal. Iranian navy ship sinks off Sri Lanka. NATO involved: Turkey shoots down Iranian missile. Iran strikes expand to Saudi Arabia, Kuwait. 6 US F-15s downed by Kuwaiti friendly fire. 9 killed in Beit Shemesh, Israel. 8 countries now involved.

item.title: Early Morning — China Slashes Growth Target, Iran War Grinds On
item.description:
Markets & Economy

  • China sets lowest GDP growth target since 1991 — target cut below "around 5%" for the first time since 2023, signaling deepening concerns over trade war fallout and domestic headwinds. BBC
  • Oil and stock markets remain volatile as fears grow that Iran conflict could drag on for weeks. Elevated energy prices threatening broader inflation. BBC
  • Venezuela cooperating with US on mining — interim president announces deal after meeting US Interior Secretary Burgum in Caracas. BBC

Iran/US Conflict

  • War enters Day 7 with no signs of slowing. Analysis pieces note "shifting stories" from Trump admin on war justification. BBC
  • Iranians describe life under constant bombardment — daily strikes, internet blackouts, security crackdowns separating families. BBC
  • UK Papers: "Trump's war goes global" — front pages across Britain focus on expanding conflict zone. BBC

AI/ML

  • TikTok refuses end-to-end encryption for DMs, claiming it would "put users at risk" — breaking from WhatsApp, Messenger approach. BBC
  • AI in hiring: can a robot judge caregivers? — AI screening tools face scrutiny as they expand into sensitive sectors. BBC

Sources: BBC News. Updated 05 Mar 2026 ~02:00 UTC.

item.title: Evening Briefing — Mar 5: Weapons Stockpile Fears, UK Evac Flights Begin, Russian Tanker Sunk, Tech Firms Flee Gulf
item.description:
Iran/Middle East — Day 6+

Weapons stockpile depletion looms: BBC defence correspondent reports depleted weapons stocks are becoming a significant factor after six days of sustained precision strikes. Both US and Israel face mounting munitions pressure. BBC

Life under constant bombardment: Iranians describe daily strikes, rolling internet blackouts, and security crackdowns. "Every day feels like a month." Millions unable to reach loved ones. BBC

First UK evacuation flights depart: 130,000+ British nationals registered to leave the Middle East. Government-chartered flights now running. HMS Dragon loading ammunition in Portsmouth, won't reach Cyprus until next week. BBC

UK political split on response: PM Starmer defends stance after Trump criticised UK for refusing base access. Badenoch calls for stronger action. BBC

Iran strikes continue: Retaliatory attacks on Israel and across the Gulf. BBC maps five days of multi-country strikes. Trump warns action could continue for weeks. BBC

Russia-Ukraine

Ukrainian drones sink Russian tanker: Arctic Metagaz went down between Libya and Malta after explosions and fire. Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones. BBC

AI / Tech

Tech giants evacuate Gulf offices: Major companies closing regional offices, shifting to remote work as war escalates. The Gulf had positioned itself as a major AI hub with billions in investment now at risk. BBC

Amazon facilities damaged by drones: Three Amazon sites in UAE and Bahrain hit, exposing tech infrastructure vulnerability. BBC

TikTok refuses end-to-end encryption: Won't implement E2E for DMs unlike WhatsApp/Messenger, citing user safety concerns. BBC

Markets / Trade

Markets volatile, oil elevated: Fears Iran war may drag on. Experts warn prolonged high energy prices will ripple through consumer goods and services. BBC

US Treasury confirms tariff hike this week: Secretary Bessent says global tariff will "likely" increase above 10% "sometime this week." BBC

US Politics

Texas primaries signal midterm direction: Results indicating which way both parties are heading as 2026 midterm season begins. BBC

Sources: BBC News. Compiled Thu, 05 Mar 2026 01:13:49 GMT

item.title: Overnight Briefing — US Sub Sinks Iranian Warship, Khamenei Funeral Postponed, Tariff Hike Imminent
item.description:
Iran / Middle East — Day 6-7

  • US submarine sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed. ~140 crew feared missing. Pentagon released torpedo impact video. (BBC)
  • Satellite images: 11+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed by US-Israeli strikes — BBC Verify.
  • Khamenei funeral postponed as bombardment continues into sixth day.
  • Weapons stockpiles depleting — BBC reports munitions becoming significant factor.
  • Iranians describe life under constant strikes — internet blackouts, security crackdowns.
  • UK: First evacuation flight departs. 130K+ Brits registered. HMS Dragon delayed until next week.
  • Starmer defends UK response after Trump criticism. Badenoch calls for more action.
  • Russia tanker Arctic Metagaz sinks in Mediterranean — Ukraine naval drones blamed.

AI / Tech

  • OpenAI modifies military deal — Altman prohibits spying on Americans. (BBC)
  • Amazon drones damage 3 facilities in UAE/Bahrain. (BBC)
  • Tech giants scramble — offices shuttered as Iran war hits Gulf AI hub. (BBC)
  • Musk trial: tells jury people read too much into his posts. (BBC)

Markets / Trade

  • Markets and oil volatile — fears war drags on. (BBC)
  • Higher tariffs THIS WEEK — Treasury Sec Bessent confirms increase above 10%. (BBC)

UK / Intelligence

  • China spying: 3 men arrested for assisting Chinese intel in UK. (BBC)

Sources: BBC News. 2026-03-05.

item.title: Evening Briefing — Iran Day 5: Warship Sunk, AI Shakeups, Markets Volatile
item.description:
Iran/US Conflict — Day 5

  • US sub sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean. ~140 crew feared missing. BBC
  • Pentagon IDs 4 of 6 US soldiers killed in Kuwait drone attack. BBC
  • Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as bombardment continues. BBC
  • 11+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed + nuclear sites hit. BBC
  • Israel evacuates southern Lebanon; 52 killed. BBC
  • Gulf states: all red lines crossed. BBC
  • Tehran food shortages feared. BBC
  • Hormuz threat — Iran threatens ships; Trump pledges Navy protection. BBC
  • Spain refuses US base access — Trump threatens embargo. BBC

AI/ML

  • First wrongful death case vs Google over Gemini. BBC
  • OpenAI amends military deal — no spying on Americans. BBC
  • Trump bans Anthropic from government. BBC
  • Meta glasses privacy scandal. BBC
  • Gulf AI hubs disrupted by Iran war. BBC
  • Block cuts thousands of jobs for AI. BBC

Markets

  • Markets volatile — oil/gas elevated. BBC
  • US tariff hike this week. BBC
  • Amazon facilities hit by Iranian drones in UAE/Bahrain. BBC

Sources: BBC News RSS feeds. Updated March 05, 2026 00:05 UTC.

item.title: Iran Conflict Day 5 — UK Evacuations Begin, Trump Policy Under Scrutiny, HMS Dragon Delayed
item.description:
Situation Update — Day 5 of US-Israeli Strikes on Iran

Strikes continue into fifth day. US-Israeli bombardment of Iran continues with no ceasefire in sight. BBC reports satellite imagery confirms extensive damage to Iranian navy and nuclear sites. Iran's weapons stockpiles assessed as significantly depleted, raising questions about its ability to sustain retaliatory operations. (BBC)

UK Response & Evacuations

First UK government evacuation flights depart Middle East. More flights planned as situation deteriorates. PM Starmer defends UK's Iran response amid pressure from opposition leader Badenoch, who calls for stronger action. UK warship HMS Dragon not expected to sail to Cyprus until next week, signaling cautious military posture. (BBC)

Trump Administration

"Shifting stories and unanswered questions." BBC analysis highlights inconsistencies in Trump administration's public narrative about the Iran campaign, including evolving justifications and unclear endgame. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed US submarine sank Iranian warship in Indian Ocean; Pentagon released torpedo strike video. (BBC)

Civilian Impact

"Every day feels like a month." Iranians describe life under what they call "constant" US-Israeli strikes. Khamenei funeral remains postponed as bombardment continues. (BBC)

Other Developments

Russia tanker sinks in Mediterranean. Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones for sinking of tanker — a separate but escalating maritime conflict. (BBC)

Sources: BBC News. Updated March 04, 2026 22:02 UTC.

item.title: Evening Escalation: Israel Hits Tehran HQ, Iran Strikes US Sites Dubai/Qatar
item.description: Israel strikes IRGC HQ in Tehran. Iran retaliates against US facilities in Dubai and Qatar. Conflict widening rapidly.

item.title: AI/ML Briefing — Open Source Explosion, $110B OpenAI Raise, Anthropic-Pentagon Standoff
item.description:
Open Source — GitHub Trending

  • agency-agents (+2,151 stars/day, 5.1k total) — Complete AI agency with specialized expert agents. Fastest growing repo.
  • Shannon (+1,847/day, 29.6k total) — Fully autonomous AI hacker. 96.15% success rate on XBOW benchmark.
  • Airi (+1,794/day, 24.5k total) — Self-hosted AI companion with voice chat. Plays Minecraft and Factorio.
  • Perplexica (+1,096/day, 30.7k total) — Open source Perplexity competitor.
  • Flowise (49.9k total) — Visual AI agent builder. De facto drag-and-drop agent platform.
  • claude-scientific-skills (+926/day) — Agent skills for research, science, finance.
  • OpenSandbox (Alibaba) — Enterprise sandbox for coding agents, GUI agents, RL training.
  • ReMe — Memory management kit for agents. Brand new.

Hugging Face — Trending Models

  • DeepSeek-R1 (685B params, 13.1k likes, 1.03M downloads) — Top reasoning model.
  • Kimi-K2.5 (1.1T params, 1.9M downloads) — Largest model on platform, multimodal.
  • Qwen domination: 12+ variants trending (0.8B-397B params). Qwen3-Coder-Next pulling 1M+ downloads.
  • GLM-5 (754B) — Strong Chinese LLM.
  • GGUF quantized models trending hard — local inference going mainstream.

Model Releases

  • GPT-5.3-Instant (March 3) — More accurate, better context, fewer caveats.
  • GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark (Feb 12) — 15x faster coding. Runs on plate-sized chips, sidesteps Nvidia.
  • Gemini 3.1 Pro (Feb 19) — Improved complex problem-solving.
  • Google Lyria 3 (Feb 18) — AI music, 30s from text prompt.

Big Money and Power Moves

  • OpenAI raised $110B from Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank.
  • OpenAI developing GitHub rival — direct competition with Microsoft, their own investor.
  • OpenAI signed Pentagon deal for military deployment with safeguards.
  • Meta-AMD chip deal worth 6 gigawatts; Meta could own 10% of AMD.
  • Nvidia dropped $4B into photonics (light-based computing).
  • Block (Dorsey) cut 40-50% workforce, going all-in on AI.

The Anthropic-Pentagon Saga

Anthropic refused Pentagon terms on lethal autonomous weapons (Feb 26). Defense Secretary designated them a "supply chain risk" (Feb 28). Trump ordered agencies to drop Claude (Feb 27). Then Claude was used in Iran strikes anyway (Feb 28). Meanwhile Claude got a memory upgrade for consumers (March 2).

Security and Risks

  • LLMs can de-anonymize pseudonymous users at scale with high accuracy.
  • AI coding bot Kiro took down AWS (Feb 20).
  • LLMs generate near-verbatim copies of novels from training data.
  • AI Wikipedia translations hallucinated sources (March 4).
  • India Supreme Court: judge cited 4 fake AI-generated legal judgments — called "misconduct."

Key Trends

1. Agent frameworks are the hottest GitHub category. 2. Chinese AI (Qwen, DeepSeek, GLM-5, Kimi) leading HuggingFace. 3. Multimodal is now default. 4. Local inference via GGUF going mainstream. 5. Security AI emerging (Shannon). 6. Coding models as separate race (Qwen3-Coder-Next).

Sources: BBC News, Ars Technica, The Verge, GitHub, Hugging Face. March 2026.

item.title: Gulf Military Situation Briefing — Hormuz Closed, IRIS Dena Sunk, Energy Infrastructure Under Fire
item.description:
Strait of Hormuz — CLOSED

IRGC Navy declared the strait under complete control of Iran's Navy. Commander Akbarzadeh stated it is "impossible for any ships to pass through." Ships warned they'll be "set ablaze."

  • 3,200 ships (4% global tonnage) idle inside the Gulf
  • ~150 tankers stranded, supertanker hire doubled to record $400,000+
  • Brent crude at $82/barrel — up 13% since conflict start
  • Strait only 33km at narrowest — entirely within Iran/Oman territorial waters
  • Backup pipelines (Saudi 5M + UAE 1.5M bbl/day) still leave 8-10M barrel/day shortfall

US Military Response — Operation "Epic Fury"

  • US submarine torpedoed and sank Iranian warship IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka — first sub torpedo attack since WWII. 80+ killed, 32 rescued, ~150 unaccounted for.
  • B-2, B-1, B-52 bombers striking missile facilities and command posts deep inside Iran
  • 20+ Iranian ships sunk total
  • 13,000+ US troops in Kuwait
  • Trump announced Navy will escort tankers through Hormuz ASAP
  • UK sending HMS Dragon to eastern Med; RAF shot down Iranian drone over Jordan
  • NATO intercepted Iranian missile heading toward Turkish airspace

Iranian Strikes Across the Region

  • US Embassies in Riyadh and Dubai struck; US Consulate in Erbil targeted
  • 500+ ballistic missiles launched (est. 3,000+ in inventory)
  • Bahrain intercepted 74 missiles + 92 drones since Saturday
  • Qatar, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman all under fire
  • 10+ oil tankers targeted; Honduran-flagged Athe Nova set ablaze

Energy Infrastructure Hit

  • Ras Tanura refinery (Saudi Aramco, 12% global oil) — operations halted
  • Ras Laffan LNG (Qatar, world's largest) — complete production halt, gas prices at 3-year high
  • Mesaieed Industrial City (Qatar) — power plant struck
  • Mussafah + Fujairah fuel terminals (UAE) damaged
  • Ports of Duqm + Salalah (Oman) fuel tanks hit

GCC Response — Defense Only

  • Qatar busted 2 IRGC sleeper cells, 10+ arrested
  • GCC refusing to let US use airspace for offensive strikes
  • Qatar FM: "All red lines have been crossed"

Casualties (March 4)

Iran: 1,045 killed. US: 6 at Port Shuaiba + 3 day 2 = 9 total. Israel: 11. Lebanon: 72 killed, 437 injured since Monday. Friendly fire: 3 US jets + 6 F-15s downed by Kuwaiti air defenses.

Watchpoints

1. US tanker escorts through Hormuz — direct confrontation trigger. 2. Oil prices if Ras Tanura + Ras Laffan stay offline. 3. Iran's 2,500+ remaining missiles. 4. Senate war powers vote (Kaine resolution).

Sources: BBC News, AP. March 3-4, 2026.

item.title: US-Israeli Strikes Hit Iranian Nuclear Sites & Navy — 11+ Vessels Destroyed
item.description:
Satellite images confirm US-Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear sites and navy. BBC Verify imagery shows 11+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed. Footage shows massive explosions across Iran. Gulf states say "all red lines crossed" — retaliatory Iranian strikes hit Gulf nations. 8+ countries now involved. Spain's PM says "No to war" — Trump threatens full trade embargo. UK evacuating nationals (flight from Oman). Oil prices rising, UK markets steadying.

Trump admin messaging under scrutiny — "shifting stories." UK PM Starmer criticized for refusing base access. Texas primaries: Talarico beats Crockett in Democratic race. Labour MP partner arrested for China espionage.

Source: BBC News RSS. 2026-03-04.

item.title: Iran Conflict Day 3 — 1000+ Dead, Markets Tumble, Mamdani Responds
item.description: Iran 1000+ dead, 6+ US KIA identified, Strait of Hormuz threatened. Markets in 3rd day of losses. NYC Mayor Mamdani calls for de-escalation. Oil spiking past $100/bbl.
